1: Vision. From chazah; a sight, i.e. A dream, revelation, or oracle.
Or mobadyahuw; active participle of abad and Yahh; serving Jah; Obadjah, the name of thirteen Israelites.
Thus, here. From the prefix k and huw'; properly, like this, i.e. By implication, thus; also here; or now.
Lord. Am emphatic form of 'adown; the Lord.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
The name of a condiment. Or oedowm; from 'adom; red; Edom, the elder twin-brother of Jacob; hence the region occupied by him.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
With (denoting proximity). Probably from 'anah; properly, nearness, near; hence, generally, with, by, at, among, etc.
A report. Feminine passive participle of shamem; something heard, i.e. An announcement.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
A hinge, a throe, a herald, erranddoer. From tsuwr; a hinge; also a throe; also a herald or errand-doer.
To send. A primitive root; to send away, for, or out.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
A battle, war. From lacham; a battle; generally, war.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
2: Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Small, young, unimportant. Or qaton; from quwt; abbreviated, i.e. Diminutive, literally or figuratively.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
Muchness, force, abundance. From the same as 'uwd; properly, vehemence, i.e. vehemently; by implication, wholly, speedily, etc.
To despise. A primitive root; to disesteem.
3: A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
To lend on interest, be a creditor. A primitive root; to lead astray, i.e. to delude, or to seduce.
A primitive root (by transmission) to shakab through the idea of lodging; compare cikluwth, shakam); to reside or permanently stay.
Places of concealment, retreats. From an unused root meaning to take refuge; a rift in rocks.
A crag, cliff. From an unused root meaning to be lofty; a craggy rock, literally or figuratively.
Seat, dwelling, place. Infinitive of yashab; properly, session; but used also concretely, an abode or locality.
Height. From ruwm; altitude, i.e. Concretely, abstractly (elation), or adverbially (aloft).
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To come or go down, descend.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
4: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To soar, be lofty, to be haughty. A primitive root; to soar, i.e. Be lofty; figuratively, to be haughty.
An eagle. From an unused root meaning to lacerate; the eagle.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
A nest. Contracted from qanan; a nest, sometimes including the nestlings; figuratively, a chamber or dwelling.
A star. Probably from the same as Kabbown or kavah; a star; figuratively, a prince.
There, thither. A primitive particle; there then; often thither, or thence.
To come or go down, descend.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
5: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
A thief. From ganab; a stealer.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
A primitive root; properly, to be burly, i.e. powerful; by implication, to ravage.
Night. Or leyl; also laylah; from the same as luwl; properly, a twist, i.e. Night; figuratively, adversity.
How?. Also eykah; and teykakah; prolonged from 'ay; how? Or how!; also where.
A primitive root; to be dumb or silent; hence, to fail or perish; trans. To destroy.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To steal. A primitive root; to thieve; by implication, to deceive.
Sufficiency, enough. Of uncertain derivation; enough, used chiefly with preposition in phrases.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To cut off, make inaccessible, enclose. A primitive root; to clip off; specifically to gather grapes; also to be isolated.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To remain, be left over. A primitive root; properly, to swell up, i.e. Be redundant.
A gleaning. Feminine active participle of alal; only in plural gleanings; by extens. Gleaning-time.
6: How?. Also eykah; and teykakah; prolonged from 'ay; how? Or how!; also where.
Apparently a form of the passive participle of asah in the original sense of handling; rough; Esav, a son of Isaac, including his posterity.
To search. A primitive root; to seek; causatively, to conceal oneself, or mask.
Hidden treasure, treasure. From tsaphan; a secret.
A primitive root; to gush over, i.e. To swell; to desire earnestly; by implication to ask.
7: The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Man, mankind. From 'anash; properly, a mortal; hence, a man in general.
A covenant. From barah (like bara'); a compact.
To send. A primitive root; to send away, for, or out.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Border, boundary, territory. Or gbul; from gabal; properly, a cord, i.e. a boundary; by extens. The territory inclosed.
Man, mankind. From 'anash; properly, a mortal; hence, a man in general.
Or shalom; from shalam; safe, i.e. well, happy, friendly; also welfare, i.e. Health, prosperity, peace.
To lend on interest, be a creditor. A primitive root; to lead astray, i.e. to delude, or to seduce.
To be able, have power. Or yakowl; a primitive root; to be able, literally or morally.
Bread, food. From lacham; food, especially bread, or grain.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Perhaps a net. From zuwr in the sense of turning aside from truth; treachery, i.e. A plot.
Underneath, below, instead of. From the same as Towach; the bottom; only adverbially, below, in lieu of, etc.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
An understanding. And tbuwnah; or towbunah; from biyn; intelligence; by implication, an argument; by extension, caprice.
8: Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
The name of a condiment. Or oedowm; from 'adom; red; Edom, the elder twin-brother of Jacob; hence the region occupied by him.
An understanding. And tbuwnah; or towbunah; from biyn; intelligence; by implication, an argument; by extension, caprice.
Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
Apparently a form of the passive participle of asah in the original sense of handling; rough; Esav, a son of Isaac, including his posterity.
9: Strong, mighty. Or gibbor; intensive from the same as geber; powerful; by implication, warrior, tyrant.
Or Teman; the same as teyman; Teman, the name of two Edomites, and of the region and descendant of one of them.
A primitive root; properly, to prostrate; hence, to break down, either by violence, or by confusion and fear.
From anah; properly, heed, i.e. Purpose; used only adverbially, on account of, teleologically, in order that.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
Apparently a form of the passive participle of asah in the original sense of handling; rough; Esav, a son of Isaac, including his posterity.
To cut off, cut down. A primitive root; to cut; by implication, to destroy or consume; specifically, to covenant.
Slaughter. From qatal; a violent death.
10: Violence, wrong. From chamac; violence; by implication, wrong; by meton. Unjust gain.
A brother. A primitive word; a brother (like 'ab).
A son of Isaac, also his desc. From aqab; heel-catcher; Jaakob, the Israelitish patriarch.
To cover. A primitive root; properly, to plump, i.e. Fill up hollows; by implication, to cover.
Shame. Feminine participle passive of buwsh; shame.
To cut off, cut down. A primitive root; to cut; by implication, to destroy or consume; specifically, to covenant.
11: Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.
From nagad; a front, i.e. Part opposite; specifically a counterpart, or mate; usually over against or before.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
A primitive root; to turn aside; hence to be a foreigner, strange, profane; specifically to commit adultery.
To take captive. A primitive root; to transport into captivity.
From chuwl; probably a force, whether of men, means or other resources; an army, wealth, virtue, valor, strength.
Foreign, alien. From neker; strange, in a variety of degrees and applications.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
A gate. From sha'ar in its original sense; an opening, i.e. Door or gate.
To cast a lot. A primitive root; properly, to handle, i.e. To throw, e.g. Lots.
Or goral; from an unused root meaning to be rough; properly, a pebble, i.e. A lot; figuratively, a portion or destiny.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Probably foundation of peace, capital city of all Isr.
One. A numeral from 'achad; properly, united, i.e. One; or first.
12: Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
A brother. A primitive word; a brother (like 'ab).
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
To act or treat as foreign or strange, to disguise. Or noker; from nakar; something strange, i.e. Unexpected calamity.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
Rejoice, be glad. A primitive root; probably to brighten up, i.e. be blithe or gleesome.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
From yadah; celebrated; Jehudah, the name of five Israelites; also of the tribe descended from the first, and of its territory.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To grow up, become great. A primitive root; properly, to twist, i.e. To be large.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Tightness, a female rival. Feminine of tsar; tightness; transitively, a female rival.
13: Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
A gate. From sha'ar in its original sense; an opening, i.e. Door or gate.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Distress, calamity. From the same as 'uwd; oppression; by implication misfortune, ruin.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.

14: Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Parting of ways, plunder. From paraq; rapine; also a fork.
To cut off, cut down. A primitive root; to cut; by implication, to destroy or consume; specifically, to covenant.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Escaped one, fugitive. Or paleyt; or palet; from palat; a refugee.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To shut, close. A primitive root; to shut up; figuratively, to surrender.
A survivor. From sarad; a survivor.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Tightness, a female rival. Feminine of tsar; tightness; transitively, a female rival.
15: Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Near. Or qarob; from qarab; near.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
A dealing, recompense, benefit. From gamal; treatment, i.e. An act; by implication, service or requital.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
16: Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To drink. A primitive root; to imbibe.

Apartness, sacredness. From qadash; a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity.

To swallow, swallow down. A primitive root; to gulp; figuratively, to be rash.

An escape. Or pletah; feminine of paliyt; deliverance; concretely, an escaped portion.

Flame, blade. Or lahebeth; feminine of lahab, and meaning the same.

Lowland. From shaphel; Lowland, i.e. the maritime slope of Palestine.

will possess the land of the Philistines.
Inhab. of Philistia. Patrial from Plesheth; a Pelishtite or inhabitant of Pelesheth.

Field, land. Or saday; from an unused root meaning to spread out; a field.

Field, land. Or saday; from an unused root meaning to spread out; a field.
Capital of N. kingdom of Isr. From the active participle of shamar; watch-station; Shomeron, a place in Palestine.
From ben and yamiyn; son of right hand; Binjamin, youngest son of Jacob; also the tribe descended from him, and its territory.

Probably from Gal'ed; Gilad, a region East of the Jordan; also the name of three Israelites.
20: An exile. Feminine from galah; captivity; concretely, exiles.
This, here. A primitive word; the masculine demonstrative pronoun, this or that.
Rampart, fortress. Or chel; a collateral form of chayil; an army; also an intrenchment.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
will possess the land of the Canaanites
Canaanite -- inhabitant of Canaan. Patrial from Kna'an; a Kenaanite or inhabitant of Kenaan; by implication, a pedlar.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Smelting place, a city S. of Sidon. From tsaraph; refinement; Tsarephath, a place in Palestine.
An exile. Feminine from galah; captivity; concretely, exiles.
Probably foundation of peace, capital city of all Isr.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
